name: markdown-mermaid-writing
tagline: Style-guide skill for writing markdown docs with embedded Mermaid diagrams, plus 24 diagram refs and 9 doc templates.
verdict: Well-structured markdown/Mermaid style-guide skill: clear triggers, actionable 5-step workflow, good pitfall/example coverage, no network calls, no secret access, no hidden instructions. Minor flags: it declares Bash tool access never used or explained (broader than needed), and metadata carries two conflicting version numbers (1.1 vs 1.0.0).

highlights.summary: allowed-tools declares Bash (line 4) alongside Read/Write/Edit, but nothing in the skill body uses, calls, or explains a need for shell execution for a markdown/diagram-writing task — capability decla

No explicit statement of the final output artifact's shape (file location/naming convention) beyond 'commit as text' (Step 5, lines 179-181); relies on implicit convention rather than a stated contrac
allowed-tools lists Bash (line 4) with no corresponding shell usage described anywhere in the skill body — declared tooling exceeds what the documented workflow needs.
